name: Ability
effect: A catch-all term for the various different skills and combat techniques characters can learn. These includes Skills, Powers, Passives, Maneuvers, Attacks, Gambits, and Reactions.

name: Action
effect: See _Camp Action_ and _Travel Action_.

name: Advantage
effect: See _Advantage (Rolling)_ or _Advantage (Skill Challenge)_.

name: Advantage (Rolling)
effect: When you roll with Advantage, roll an additional die then choose which die or dice to resolve the result with. For example, in an _Attack Roll_, you would roll 3d6, then choose which 2 die to use.

name: Advantage (Skill Challenge)
effect: Advantage is an _Augment_ that can be purchased in a _Skill Challenge_. When you purchase it, you set things up to aid yourself or another in the future, creating a condition or augmented opportunity.

name: Affinity
effect: Affinity is a measurement of how well-liked the party is by the spirits of a travel Leg. Typically, this will be a three to four segment Clock that is filled when the party makes offerings or otherwise assists the local spirits. Whenever they are on that Leg characters may clear segments on the clock for +1 die on a Travel Roll or Skill Challenge as the local spirits provide some small assistance.

name: Affliction
effect: Advantage is a potential _Danger_ in a _Skill Challenge_. If not removed, you typically gain a condition or a wound which can impose a die penalty or deny some actions.

name: Area
effect: Some effects, rather than targeting a single creature, will target an Area. These target all valid creatures within the listed Area, typically a single _Engagement_ or _Arena_.

name: Arena
effect: Arenas are distinct areas on a battlefield that take some significant effort to traverse between and designate different areas of combat. Generally, characters may spend  Movement to move from an Arena to any connected Arenas.

name: Armament
effect: This designates this as an Ability that typically targets Equipment you have Worn or Equipped.

name: Armor
effect: Armor is a specific type of _Equipment_ that grants bonuses when _Worn_. Armored plating, high-quality boots, or a traveling cloak are all different types of armor that a character can benefit from.

name: Armored
effect: This keyword simply designates that this Trait grants some form of natural armor; creatures may only have a single Trait with this keyword.

name: Attack
effect: When a creature makes an Attack they roll 2d6 and consult the Attack's _Profile_ (often called the Attack Roll) to determine the effect. On a _Miss_, creatures gain _Momentum_ equal to half of the total cost of the Attack (rounded down), including any purchased _Enhancements_.

name: Augments
effect: The various things that can be used to increase the degree of success or add additional effects to a _Skill Challenge_.

name: Bend
effect:
Characters with bend for an element gain the following benefits when targeting _Equipment_ of that type:
* You may use [_Armament_] Abilities (such as _Draw/Stow_) at the listed Bend Range. If they would normally create an [_Opening_], they instead do not.
* You may use _Interact_ at the listed Bend Range when targeting the appropriate material.

name: Block
effect: When a character takes Damage, they may gain _Guard_ rather than _Stamina_ for an amount of Damage equal to their Block.

name: Blood Loss
effect: You've lost a significant amount of blood, making you light-headed an limiting your ability to act. This _Wound_ 1 sections of your _Health Bar_ and has a 3 Segment Clock to heal. While you have this _Wound_, lose 3 _Guard_ at _Respite_. You may not mark progress on any other _Wound_ until this _Wound_ is healed, but, once per Camp Phase, you may automatically mark a segment on its Clock by eating an additional portion of Food.

name: Boldly
effect: When a character rolls Boldly during a Skill Challenge they are focusing on succeeding and throwing caution to the wind. After initially rolling, a character rerolls all [-] and [ ] once.

name: Bond
effect: Characters should track bonds between themselves and other characters; when the other character is present, characters can call in a Bond as a Condition for a bonus on a roll. If the other member of the Bond is a player, they instead may help on a Skill Challenge without taking a _Stress_. After using a Bond, reduce it by one rank.

name: Bonus
effect: When an Attack gains Bonus Damage or Guard, distribute it between targets however you wish.

name: Breakable
effect: Traits with this tag may be _Shattered_ as if they were _Equipment_; they have an effective _Durability_ equal to their Breakable value.

name: Camp Action
effect:
Camp Actions are all small things that need to be done around the camp, or can help a character properly recover. Each character can normally do one per Camp Phase. In general, basic tasks such as breaking camp, making a fire, and setting up tents are done without a character using their Camp Action. Common Camp Actions are as follows:
* **Cook:** Much of the food brought on journeys doesn't requires cooking, but game caught on the trail or other similar foods must be cooked to be safely eaten. This action requires Cooking Tools and a fire.
* **Cover:** You take the time to hide traces of the camp, which may provide an Advantage.
* **Forage/Hunt:** As the options during Travel, but treat yourself as having placed 0 die on the effect, causing you to roll three dice and take the worst two.
* **Mend:** Choose a piece of Equipment that is missing _Durability_ but not _Destroyed_. If you have the appropriate tools, roll a d6. On a 4+, restore one _Durability_. Only one character can target the same item, and this requires the appropriate tools for working with that material.
* **Patch:** Mark off a medicine. You or another character roll an additional die on their _Rest Roll_, and may immediately mark one segment on a Wound Clock. A character may only benefit from this once per Camp.
* **Rest:** You and your Spirit gain an additional die on your _Rest Roll_.
* **Secure:** You take the time to fortify the camp, which may provide an Advantage.
* **Vent:** Choose another character; the two of you roleplay a quick scene; afterwards, both of you clear 1 **Stress** and gain a shared _Bond_. This requires both of your _Camp Actions_.
* **Channel:** You may only take this action if you have considerable _Source_ for your Spirit. Your spirit rolls an 2 die during its _Rest Roll_.
* **Pamper:** You spend time with your spirit and taking care of it, whether that be playing with it, styling it, or something similar. Afterwards, you or your spirit clears 1 _Stress_. If you have an appropriate item, repeat the effect.

name: Cautiously
effect: When a character rolls Cautiously during a Skill Challenge they are focusing on avoiding harm and keeping themselves safe. After initially rolling, a character rerolls all [+] and [ ] once.

name: Climb
effect: Sheer cliff faces and similar terrain require creatures to climb. While Climbing (including entering an Arena where you must Climb), all Movement costs are increased by 2, and creatures must have two _Free Hands_ (or equivalent) to move. Any other _Movement_ when a creature must _Climb_ has its cost increased by 1. Climbing creatures have _Disadvantage_ on all rolls, and rolls against them have _Advantage_ as they attempt to cling to the surface, and must have at least one _Free Hand_ to maintain their grip. Creatures with the _Climber_ trait or similar ignore all these penalties.

name: Close
effect: Close Range effects may target any valid target within your current _Arena_.

name: Cover
effect:
While in Cover:
* Non-Melee Attacks against you have _Disadvantage_.
* You reduce the cost of all _Dodge_ _Reactions_ by 1.
* When Attacked, gain additional _Block_ equal to twice the Range the Attack was made from.

name: Crush
effect:
Damage that deals blunt trauma such as hammers, unarmed strikes, and flung stones. If a character is _Downed_ by Crush Damage they suffer the below effects:
* **6-12**: Gain a _Minor Contusion_.
* **3-5**: Gain a _Minor Contusion_, then roll a d6. On a 6, gain a _Minor Concussion_, otherwise, randomly determine which of your limbs or is damaged, taking a Minor Injury for it (typically a _Minor Leg Injury_ or _Minor Arm Injury_).
* **2-**: Gain a _Minor Contusion_, then roll a d6. On a 6, gain a _Moderate Concussion_, otherwise, randomly determine which of your limbs or is damaged, taking a Minor Injury for it (typically a _Moderate Leg Injury_ or _Moderate Arm Injury_).

name: Dangers
effect: The various things that can go wrong or make the situation worse when you make a _Skill Challenge_.

name: Defend
effect: Different abilities may allow a creature to Defend. When a creature Defends, they roll 2d6, consult the chart associated with the Ability, and determine the result (Miss, Graze, Hit, Crit). They then compare this result to the result of the incoming attack and determine if they rolled lower (a Loss), matched the Attacker (Tie), or exceeded the Attacker (Win).

name: Discount
effect: Reduce the Cost of the applicable effect by the listed Discount; this may apply to _Movement_, _Momentum_, or _Guard_. If this reduces the Cost below 0, you may spend any remainder on Enhancements for the applicable attack. Any remaining _Discount_ is lost.

name: Disengage
effect: When a character Disengages you must stop _Occupying_ your current _Terrain_. Additionally, you may choose to remove _Engaged_ (if applicable) and leave your current _Field_ effect (if applicable).

name: Dodge
effect: You defend yourself by attempting to get out of the way of an incoming assault.

name: Effective
effect: If an effect is Effective, increase the final roll of the Attack by 1 and add [_Pierce_ 2]. Additionally, the target gains -1 to _Negate_ against the effect.

name: Enhancement
effect: When a character uses an Ability they may choose a single Enhancement, which is typically a way for a character to increase the effectiveness of an Enhancement through spending additional resources.

name: Essence
effect: Essence is used to fuel a character's Craft, and is a representation of their current elemental attunement. Unlike _Momentum_, Essence is not lost at _Respite_, and a character and their spirit share the same pool of Essence. Characters typically gain a small amount of Essence at _Respite_, but can also gain additional through techniques like _Spiritcraft_.

name: Exhaust
effect: After using any Ability with this keyword, that Ability may not be used for the remainder of the combat.

name: Fall
effect: Fall Damage ignores all _Block_, but characters may reduce the Damage dealt by their _Reflex_. If a character takes at least 4 Damage they fall _Prone_.

name: Fertile
effect: Any Bloomcraft Techniques that target this _Arena_ or creatures within it have their _Cost_ reduced by 1, to a minimum of 1.

name: Final
effect: After using this Ability, end your Turn.

name: Fire
effect:
Damage dealt by flames, heat, and other similar effects. If a character is _Downed_ by Fire Damage they suffer the below effects:
* **6-12**: Gain a _Minor Burn_.
* **3-5**: Gain a _Moderate Burn_.
* **2-**: Gain a _Major Burn_.

name: Fleeting
effect: Fleeting Terrain is _Destroyed_ after _Respite_ at the start of the next _Round_.

name: Foci
effect: While _Wielding_ this, whenever you you use an effect that allows you to place _Momentum_ on an Art or Technique and you choose an Art or Technique that matches this Foci's type, place an additional _Momentum_ and treat the Hands used to _Wield_ this as Free.

name: Follow-Up
effect: Attacks with this Keyword typically are triggered by some other effect and may not qualify for _Gambits_.

name: Food
effect:
Characters must eat Food every day or gain _Hunger_. Food has levels of quality, outline below:
* <u>Nauseating</u>: Characters gain a _Stress_ for eating this.
* <u>Bad</u>: After eating this, roll a d6. On a 3 or less, gain a _Stress_.
* <u>Basic</u>: Eating this never adds nor removes Stress.
* <u>Good</u>: After eating this, roll a d6. On a 4 or higher, clear a _Stress_.
* <u>Delicious</u>: After eating this, clear a _Stress_.

name: Gambit
effect: Gambits are _Maneuvers_ that allow characters to enhance an Attack or Defend. When making an Attack, characters may use a Gambit if they roll doubles or roll a Critical Hit. If Defending, characters may use a Gambit if they roll doubles or Win. Characters may qualify for more than one Gambit on a single roll, such as when they roll doubles and get a Critical Hit. If a Gambit is triggered by an _Area_ Attack resolve the effects against a single Target per Gambit unless specified otherwise.

name: Glancing
effect: Attacks with this keyword treat the Target's _Block_ as twice its value. Apply this after _Pierce_.

name: Guard
effect: Characters have Guard, which represents their ability to completely absorb or negate attacks. When a character takes Damage, they may take an amount equal to their _Block_ to Guard, losing Guard instead of _Stamina_.

name: Hand-and-a-Half
effect: This _Equipment_ may be _Wielded_ in one or both Hands.

name: Ignite
effect: Whenever an effect with Ignite targets something with the _Flammable_ keyword it becomes _Blazing_. If an entire _Arena_ is Flammable a single _Engagement_ becomes _Blazing_, but it may spread to the rest of the _Arena_.

name: Ineffective
effect: If an effect is Ineffective, decrease the final roll all Attacks by 1. Additionally, the target gains +2 _Block_ and +1 to _Negate_ against the effect.

name: Inspiring
effect: This designates this as a support Ability that typically requires calling out to allies.

name: Instinctive
effect: This Ability may always be used regardless of your current Stance and its limitations, but may only be used after attempting to the best of your ability what your Stance demands. Typically, this is only relevant for NPCs and companion spirits.

name: Limited
effect: This ability may only be used once in the listed frequency, typically either _Turn_, _Round_, or _Phase_.

name: Long
effect: Long Range effects may target any valid target in an _Arena_ three away.

name: Lore
effect: Lore may be spent whenever you come across something in the world. If you do, you instantly learn some relevant and helpful knowledge. You regain your Lore whenever you take Downtime within a location with books or similar material. The information granted will not be as precise as somebody with actual expertise, but always provides immediately helpful information. If there is no helpful information to provide, do not expend a use.

name: Melee
effect: Melee effects may only be used against creatures currently in a _Engagement_ with you.

name: Missile
effect: Many abilities require a Missile, or a specific item or equipment. These abilities will specify any requirements on the Missile (such as an Elemental type or Size) and within what Range it must be. This ability uses said Missile to resolve the Attack, placing it unattended in target's _Arena_ or, if it _Wounds_ them, is _Impaled_ in the Target. To be used as Missile, an item must be _Worn_ by a willing Target or unattended. An exception is if a creature is _Impaled_ by an item; this counts as a _Worn_ item by the creature but may be targeted as if it was _Unattended_. Any Attacks using an _Impaled_ item against that creature have _Advantage_, ignore _Guard_, and cannot be _Defended_ against.

name: Momentum
effect:
Momentum represents your ability to act and fight in combat. It is spent for a myriad of effects, but most commonly increasing the power of abilities using Enhancements and paying for other Abilities. At the start of each Round characters lose all their previous _Momentum_ (gaining 2 _Guard_ for each _Momentum_ lost) then gain _Momentum_ as dictated by their _Stance_.

If a creature would lose Momentum due to an effect but has no Momentum or is an NPC they instead gain _Impaired_.

name: Movement
effect:
A resource representing how mobile a character is, and how much they can move about the battlefield. Characters may always spend _Movement_ in the following ways on their _Turn_:
* 1 _Movement_: If not _Engaged_, become _Engaged_ with a creature in your _Arena_.
* 1 _Movement_: Remove _Engaged_ as well as any _Terrain Bonus_ or _Hazard_ you have.
* 2 _Movement_: If not _Engaged_, move to an adjacent _Arena_.
* 1 _Movement_: _Occupy_ _Terrain_ or a _Hazard_. You automatically become _Engaged_ with any other creatures _Occupying_ the same effect.
* 1 _Movement_: If not _Engaged_, take a specific position within your _Arena_, the effects of which should be resolved with your GM. For example, you might move to block a piece of unattended Equipment, which would require that any creature that wants to grab it become _Engaged_ with you.

name: Occupy
effect: While a creature is Occupying Terrain they gain the listed effects of said Terrain. A number of creatures may Occupy a Terrain equal to its Size. If a Terrain is Forcibly Moved, all creatures Occupying it stop Occupying.

name: Opening
effect: When a creature creates an Opening they are doing something that compromises their defenses. Many _Punish_ _Reactions_ allow enemies to _Attack_ when an Opening is created.

name: Pacifist
effect: If you use this Maneuver you may not use any Attacks this _Turn_.

name: Parry
effect: You defend yourself by attempting to intercept, block, or otherwise deflect an attack. If the Attack is not _Melee_ you have _Disadvantage_ on the _Defense Roll_ if you are not _Wielding_ any [_Shielding_] _Equipment_.

name: Pierce
effect: Attacks with _Pierce_ ignore an amount of the Target's _Block_ equal to their Pierce value.

name: Poison
effect:
Attacks that inflict toxins or other noxious substances on a character. Typically this is biological warfare, but poisons can be formulated that affect spirits using their weaknesses to other elements. If a character is _Downed_ by Poison Damage they may suffer the below _Wounds_:
* **6-12**: Gain _Envenomed_.
* **3-5**: Gain _Envenomed_ twice.
* **2-**: Gain _Envenomed_ thrice.

name: Posture
effect: Posture represents a character's ability to parry, dodge, and otherwise avoid incoming assaults. It is commonly used for _Dodge_ and _Parry_ Reactions.

name: Priority
effect: This Action must be used immediately after a _Phase_ begins and ignores the normal _Initiative_ process.

name: Quickdraw
effect: So long as your have a _Free Hand_ you may always treat this piece of Equipment as _Wielded_ while it is _Worn_.

name: Rank
effect: Rank refers to the result of an Attack or Defense Roll and corresponds to Miss, Graze, Hit, and Crit, in that order. Many effects increase or decrease the Rank, which moves it up or down the chart as applicable. An effect may not go below a Miss or above a Crit.

name: Reach
effect: Reach effects are treated like a _Melee_ effect, regardless of Range.

name: Reactive
effect: This Enhancement may be selected at any point in an effect, even after dice have been rolled.

name: Recover
effect: When a creature Recovers they may remove one of their Statuses, as outlined by its Recover text. For many Statuses this will simply remove the effect (such as _Impaired_) but other Statuses may require a roll or additional action (such as _Alight_).

name: Remote
effect: This Attack does not originate from the attacker; when determining _Cover_ and effects such as _Push_ use the origin of the attack, not the attacker.

name: Repeatable
effect: This Ability may be used any number of times on a given Turn.

name: Repelled
effect: If an effect is Repelled, decrease the final Rank of all Attacks by 1. Additionally, the target gains +4 _Block_ and +2 to _Negate_ against the effect.

name: Retreat
effect:
Retreat is a special movement pattern used by NPCs. When a creature Retreats, they first leave their current _Engagement_, and may use any abilities that prevent an _Opening_. They may end their _Movement_ there, or may either:
* Become _Engaged_ with an ally.
* _Occupy_ _Terrain_.
* Move to an _Arena_ with no enemies.

Regardless of what they choose, they may not willingly _Engage_ an enemy under any circumstances.

name: Rooted
effect: You may not use _Movement_ this _Turn_.

name: Rough
effect: This designates an area that is difficult to move in, whether due to uneven ground or other limitations. _Movement_ Costs in this _Arena_ are increased by 1 and _Negate_ _Reflex_ with _Disadvantage_. If multiple effects can make an Arena _Rough_ the effects do not stack.

name: Safe
effect: Any Attacks made as a part of a [_Punish_] _Reaction_ are made at _Disadvantage_ against you this _Turn_.

name: Short
effect: Short Range effects may target any valid target within an adjacent _Arena_.

name: Siege
effect: Optionally, when making this Attack against a creature _Occupying_ _Terrain_, the _Terrain_ first loses 1 _Hardness_. If this destroys the _Terrain_, the Target does not benefit from any effects it was granting. If you Attack the Obstacle directly it always loses 1 additional _Hardness_ and you treat all _Stun_ inflicted as _Damage_.

name: Skill Check
effect: When a skill check is called for in combat, the character will instead roll their dice pool and count the number of [+].  Characters do not reroll due to attempting something _Cautiously_, _Boldly_, or _Skillfully_ in an this circumstance. If a character achieves enough [+] they succeed; otherwise, they fail. Typically, there is no penalty for failing beyond the resources spent to attempt this.

name: Slash
effect:
Damages that rips, slashes, or tears, commonly represented by claws and blades. If a character is _Downed_ by Slash Damage they suffer the below effects:
* **6-12**: Gain a _Minor Flesh Wound_.
* **3-5**: Gain a _Minor Flesh Wound_, then randomly determine which of your limbs is damaged, taking a Minor Injury for it (typically a _Minor Leg Injury_ or _Minor Arm Injury_).
* **2-**:  Gain a _Minor Flesh Wound_, then randomly determine which of your limbs is damaged, taking a Moderate Injury for it (typically a _Moderate Leg Injury_ or _Moderate Arm Injury_).

name: Slow Turn
effect: A Slow _Turn_ happens after all other _Turn_ in a _Phase_. If there are multiple Slow Turns, resolve them in popcorn Initiative as normal.

name: Small
effect: This piece of Equipment may be equipped in a consumables Slot. If it is, it costs no _Load_.

name: Soft Cover
effect: While in Soft Cover Non-Melee Attacks against you have _Disadvantage_.

name: Source
effect: Sources are strong concentrations of elemental power or material that can easily be controlled by spiritcraft. Sources are generally categorized as *Least*, *Minor*, *Moderate*, *Major*, and *Greater*, but may just be referred to as a Source when the severity does not matter. Different abilities typically require Sources, either to power their spiritcraft or simply fling with kinetic force. Items and other objects within the world will specify what type and rank of Source they are. Typically, sources are the standard elemental types, but other sources (such as Plant) might be referenced by other abilities.

name: Spirit
effect: This Ability makes use of your spiritcraft; the Cost increases by 1 if you are _Suppressed_, and cannot be used if you are _Sealed_.

name: Stab
effect:
Damages that pierces, such as arrows, fangs, and spears. If a character is _Downed_ by Stab Damage they suffer the below effects:
* **6-12**: Gain a _Minor Flesh Wound_.
* **3-5**: Gain a _Moderate Flesh Wound_.
* **2-**: Gain a _Major Flesh Wound_.

name: Stack
effect: A single Load of this item is comprised of multiple copies, designated by the Stack value. Each individual copy is Load 0.

name: Stun
effect: Stun is a specialized type of Damage that is always applied to posture, never to Stamina. Inflicted Stun it is always applied before other Damage.

name: Summon
effect:
When a spirit is Summoned they take physical form until _Defeated_ or _Recalled_, and use the following rules:
* Spirits have their own Stamina, Guard, and Movement, and gain their own Status effects.
* Spirits and their companion share _Momentum_.
* Spirits act at their listed _Speed_ during the players' phase.

name: Super Effective
effect: If an effect is Super Effective, increase the final rank of the Attack by 1 and add [_Pierce_ 4]. Additionally, the target gains -2 to _Negate_ against the effect.

name: Swim
effect: When in areas with deep water creatures must swim. Creatures without the _Swimmer_ trait must pay an additional _Movement_ for all effects while Swimming except entering the _Arena_. Additionally, while Swimming a creature immediately becomes _Soaked_.

name: Temporary
effect: Temporary bonuses and resources are are lost at the end of the current _Phase_. Temporary bonuses do not count against any caps that a character might have.

name: Thrown
effect: After using an effect with this Range, place this _Equipment_ in the Target's _Arena_.

name: Unblockable
effect: Creatures have _Disadvantage_ on any _Parry_ effect against this Attack, and cannot use _Defensive Gambits_.

name: Undodgeable
effect: Creatures have _Disadvantage_ on any _Dodge_ effect against this Attack, and cannot use _Defensive Gambits_.

name: Ward
effect: Characters reduce all Damage and Stun inflicted by appropriate effects by their Ward value.

name: Weakness and Resistance
effect: This represents some resilience or innate resistance to a type or effect. Effects will either be _Super Effective_, _Effective_, _Neutral_, _Ineffective_ or _Repelled_. For each Resistance a creature has, move the effect towards _Repelled_; for each Weakness, move it towards _Super Effective_. Weakness and Resistance cancel each other out at a 1 to 1 rate.

name: Weaponsmith's Blessing
effect:
A Weaponsmith's Blessing reflects gear that has been carefully and dutifully maintained, and can be expended for the following effects:
* In a Skill Challenge, when the weapon is granting a die, you may gain an additional die.
* After making an Attack with a Weapon, you may expend the blessing to use a _Gambit_, even if you didn't qualify for one.
* When a Weapon would lose _Durability_, you may expend the Weaponsmith's Blessing to instead not lose _Durability_.

name: Wielded
effect: Equipment that is Wielded is actively being held in a character's hands and can be fully utilized. Characters typically have two hands to _Wield_ things with.

name: Worn
effect: Equipment that a character is wearing, whether that be armor that is actively worn or a potion on their belt. Worn items can be easily accessed in combat and other challenges.
